producer.name: Domaine de la Romanée-Conti
producer.philosophy: The most prestigious wine estate in the world. Monopole owner of Romanée-Conti and La Tâche — two of Burgundy's greatest Grand Cru vineyards. Biodynamic since 1985, with horse-drawn plowing of the most precious parcels. Whole-cluster fermentation, indigenous yeasts, long maceration, and aging in new French oak define a house style of extraordinary purity and complexity. Production is tiny — Romanée-Conti itself yields only ~450 cases per year from 1.81 hectares.

metadata.owners: de Villaine and Leroy/Roch families (co-owners since 1942)
metadata.co_manager: Bertrand de Villaine (since 2018, succeeded Aubert de Villaine)
metadata.viticulture: Fully biodynamic. Selection massale from estate vines. Horse-drawn plowing in Romanée-Conti and La Tâche. Yields kept extremely low (20-30 hl/ha). No herbicides or pesticides.
metadata.vineyard_notes: 25.5 hectares across 8 Grand Cru and 1 Premier Cru vineyard in the Côte de Nuits and Côte de Beaune. Romanée-Conti (1.81 ha, monopole), La Tâche (6.06 ha, monopole), Richebourg (3.51 ha), Romanée-Saint-Vivant (5.29 ha), Grands Échezeaux (3.53 ha), Échezeaux (4.67 ha), Corton (2.27 ha, added 2009), Montrachet (0.67 ha), Vosne-Romanée 1er Cru (parcels from Petits Monts, Cuvée Duvault-Blochet).
metadata.replanting_history: Romanée-Conti vineyard replanted in 1947 (produced no wine 1946-1951). Last vintage from pre-phylloxera vines: 1945.

metadata.title: Winemaker
metadata.notes: First female winemaker at DRC. Previously at Domaine de la Vougeraie.

metadata.title: Winemaker (retired)
metadata.notes: Winemaker for 38 vintages. Joined as cellar assistant in 1971, became head winemaker in 1985. Oversaw the transition to biodynamic farming.

wines.name: Romanée-Conti Grand Cru
wines.vinification_notes: 100% whole-cluster fermentation. Indigenous yeasts, long cold soak, gentle extraction during 18-21 day maceration. Aged 16-20 months in 100% new French oak (François Frères). Unfined, unfiltered. Monopole: 1.81 hectares, ~450 cases annually.

metadata.soil: Bathonian limestone with clay and marl. Thin topsoil over fractured bedrock. Mid-slope at 260m elevation.

wines.name: La Tâche Grand Cru
wines.vinification_notes: Typically 80-100% whole-cluster fermentation (varies by vintage). Indigenous yeasts, 18-21 day maceration. Aged 16-20 months in 100% new French oak. Unfined, unfiltered. Monopole: 6.06 hectares, ~1,500 cases annually.

metadata.soil: Varied terroir across the vineyard. Upper slope: thin clay over limestone. Mid-slope: deeper marl and clay. Lower slope: richer alluvial deposits. Elevation 255-300m.
metadata.notes: Originally just the La Tâche climat (1.44 ha). Extended in 1933 to include Les Gaudichots (4.62 ha). DRC monopole since 1933.

wines.name: Richebourg Grand Cru
wines.vinification_notes: Typically 70-90% whole-cluster fermentation. Indigenous yeasts, 18-21 day maceration. Aged 16-20 months in 100% new French oak. Unfined, unfiltered. 3.51 hectares across two parcels (Richebourg and Les Verroilles), ~1,000 cases annually.

metadata.soil: Deep clay and marl over Bajocian limestone. Rich, well-drained soils. Mid-slope at 260-280m.

wines.name: Romanée-Saint-Vivant Grand Cru
wines.vinification_notes: Typically 50-80% whole-cluster fermentation (lower stem inclusion than La Tâche or Romanée-Conti). Indigenous yeasts, 18-21 day maceration. Aged 16-20 months in 100% new French oak. Unfined, unfiltered. 5.29 hectares, ~1,500 cases annually.

metadata.soil: Lighter soils than Romanée-Conti. Bathonian limestone with less clay. Well-drained. Lower on the slope at 250-260m.
metadata.notes: Previously leased from Marey-Monge family (1966-1988). DRC acquired the vines outright in 1988. The vineyard was named for the Priory of Saint-Vivant de Vergy, which owned it from the 13th century.

wines.name: Grands Échezeaux Grand Cru
wines.vinification_notes: Typically 60-80% whole-cluster fermentation. Indigenous yeasts, 18-21 day maceration. Aged 16-20 months in 100% new French oak. Unfined, unfiltered. 3.53 hectares, ~1,000 cases annually.

metadata.soil: Oolitic limestone with clay. Higher proportion of limestone than Échezeaux. Upper slope position at 260-280m.

wines.name: Échezeaux Grand Cru
wines.vinification_notes: Typically 50-70% whole-cluster fermentation. Indigenous yeasts, 18-21 day maceration. Aged 16-20 months in 100% new French oak. Unfined, unfiltered. 4.67 hectares across multiple lieux-dits, ~1,200 cases annually.

metadata.soil: Variable across the large vineyard. DRC parcels primarily in upper Échezeaux with more limestone.
metadata.notes: Largest Grand Cru in terms of DRC holdings. Often considered the most approachable DRC wine in youth.

wines.name: Corton Grand Cru
wines.vinification_notes: DRC's newest wine, first vintage 2009. Sourced from three lieux-dits: Le Clos du Roi, Les Bressandes, and Les Renardes. Typically 50-70% whole-cluster fermentation. Indigenous yeasts, 16-18 day maceration. Aged 16-20 months in 100% new French oak. Unfined, unfiltered. 2.27 hectares, ~700 cases annually.

metadata.soil: Iron-rich clay and limestone on the hill of Corton. Easterly and southerly exposures at 280-330m.
metadata.notes: Prince de Mérode vines, acquired in 2008. First vintage 2009. Only Grand Cru in the Côte de Beaune for red wine.

wines.name: Montrachet Grand Cru
wines.vinification_notes: DRC's only white wine. Extraordinarily rare — approximately 2-3 barrels (50-75 cases) per vintage from 0.67 hectares. Whole-cluster pressed, fermented and aged 16-20 months in 100% new French oak. Indigenous yeasts. No bâtonnage. Bottled unfined and unfiltered.

metadata.soil: Limestone and marl. DRC parcel sits on the Puligny side, mid-slope at 250-260m. Thin topsoil over Bathonian limestone.
metadata.notes: One of the rarest and most expensive white wines in the world. The vineyard straddles Puligny-Montrachet and Chassagne-Montrachet; DRC's parcel is on the Puligny side.

wines.name: Vosne-Romanée Premier Cru Cuvée Duvault-Blochet
wines.vinification_notes: DRC's newest cuvée, first vintage 2019. Blend of several Premier Cru parcels in Vosne-Romanée, including Les Petits Monts, Les Reignots, and Les Suchots. Named for Jacques-Marie Duvault-Blochet, who assembled the DRC estate in the 19th century. Typically 50-70% whole-cluster fermentation. Aged 16-20 months in new French oak. Unfined, unfiltered.

metadata.notes: Created from declassified Premier Cru parcels that were previously blended into the Grand Cru wines. Named in homage to Jacques-Marie Duvault-Blochet (1796-1874), who purchased Romanée-Conti in 1869 and assembled most of the domaine's current holdings.
